task: Implement a production-grade real-time truck blind spot monitoring dashboard. Replace ALL mock/simulated data with live WebSocket data from the backend. Preserve the exact UI design spec described below.

tech_stack.renderer: Three.js via direct import (import * as THREE from 'three')
tech_stack.websocket: Native browser WebSocket API — do NOT use socket.io or any library
tech_stack.fonts: Google Fonts — Barlow, Barlow Condensed, JetBrains Mono (loaded via @import in CSS string)
tech_stack.state: React hooks only (useState, useEffect, useRef, useCallback)
tech_stack.no_external_ui_libs: Do not use any UI component libraries (no MUI, no Ant Design, no Radix)

websocket_integration.message_format: JSON — each message is a UTF-8 string parsed with JSON.parse()

on_message: Increment message counter ref for FPS calculation
If any detection.camera_zone is in ['left','right','rear'], set alertActive=true and schedule setAlertActive(false) after 3000ms via setTimeout (clear previous timeout first)
If navigator.vibrate is available, call navigator.vibrate(200) on blind spot detections
connection_lifecycle.on_close: Set isConnected = false. Schedule reconnect attempt after 3000ms.
connection_lifecycle.reconnect_logic: Attempt reconnect after 3s on close. Expose a manual reconnect function called by the Reconnect button.
connection_lifecycle.cleanup: Close WebSocket on component unmount or on server IP change

fps_calculation.method: Count WebSocket messages received in a rolling 1-second window
fps_calculation.implementation: Use a useRef counter (msgCountRef). Every 1000ms via setInterval, read the counter, set fps state, reset counter to 0.
fps_calculation.display: '{n} FPS' — red if fps < 15, dim gray if fps < 20, white if fps >= 20

server_ip_state.configurable_via: SettingsModal — user types a new IP, clicks 'Apply & Reconnect', WebSocket closes and reopens to new IP
server_ip_state.validation: Accept IPv4 (e.g. 192.168.1.100), 'localhost', or valid hostnames. Reject invalid strings.
server_ip_state.display: Show '{serverIP} : 8081' in the connection pill in the header

design_system.theme: Dark monochrome. Pure blacks and grays only. Zero color except --red for danger states.
design_system.accent_rule: #cc2222 red is used EXCLUSIVELY for: active alert banner background, blind-spot zone card borders, blind-spot zone wireframe in 3D scene, detection blip markers for blind zones, BLIND SPOT tab active state, status strip danger values. Everything else is white/gray/black.
design_system.no_colored_lights_in_three_scene: Use only white/neutral lights in Three.js — no blue ambient, no cyan/pink point lights
design_system.borders: 1px solid var(--line) everywhere. Cards 8px border-radius. Header buttons 5px. Pills 9999px.
design_system.typography_rules: All section labels and stat labels: font-family var(--fc), UPPERCASE, letter-spacing 2px+, font-weight 700, color var(--t3). All live numeric values: font-family var(--fm). Body/descriptions: var(--fu).

layout.root: flex column, height 100vh, overflow hidden, background var(--bg-0)
stack: 3. Main Body — flex 1, overflow hidden, flex row: [Scene (flex 1) | Side Panel (width 290px)]
layout.alert_banner: position absolute on the header, slides down from top on alertActive=true, full-width red bar

left.logo: Text 'SafeDetect' — font-family var(--fc), font-size 22px, font-weight 700, letter-spacing 3px, uppercase. Preceded by a 26x26px bordered box with letter 'S' inside (border 2px solid white, border-radius 4px).

center.camera_nav: 4 toggle buttons inside a pill container: '360°' (id: default) | 'REAR' | 'LEFT' | 'RIGHT'. Active button: background var(--t0), color var(--bg-0). Inactive: transparent bg, color var(--t2). Clicking a view button updates the Three.js camera to a preset angle.

right.connection_pill: Shows '{serverIP} : 8081'. Green animated dot when connected (animation: opacity pulse 2s). Gray static dot when disconnected.
right.reconnect_button: Icon-only button (↺ symbol), 34x34px. Calls manual reconnect function.

StatusStrip.item_style: flex row, align-center, gap 10px, padding 0 22px, border-right 1px solid var(--line). Label: var(--fc) 10px 600 letter-spacing 2px uppercase var(--t3). Value: var(--fm) 13px 700 var(--t0).

AlertBanner.position: position absolute, top 0, left 0, right 0, z-index 200 — renders inside the header's relative container so it slides down over the scene
AlertBanner.animation: transform translateY(-100%) when hidden, translateY(0) when visible — transition 0.2s ease
AlertBanner.appearance: background var(--red), flex row center, gap 12px, padding 11px 24px
AlertBanner.content: Two blinking white dots + text 'BLIND SPOT ALERT — OBSTACLE DETECTED'
AlertBanner.blink_dot: 8px white circle, animation: opacity blinks at 0.6s step-end

ThreeJsScene.canvas: Native canvas element, fills parent div (width 100%, height 100%), cursor grab
ThreeJsScene.renderer: THREE.WebGLRenderer antialias=true, setClearColor(0x080808), shadowMap PCFSoft enabled, pixelRatio capped at 2
lights: DirectionalLight(0xffffff, 1.3) at position (8,14,6), castShadow=true, shadow camera ±12
DirectionalLight(0xffffff, 0.35) at position (-6,6,-8) — fill light, no shadow
ThreeJsScene.ground: PlaneGeometry(50,50) rotation.x=-PI/2, MeshStandardMaterial color 0x0d0d0d roughness 1, receiveShadow=true

orbit_controls.implementation: Manual mouse/touch orbit — NO OrbitControls import needed
orbit_controls.state: useRef with { theta, phi, r, drag, lastX, lastY }
orbit_controls.mouse_drag: theta -= dx*0.006, phi clamped [0.18, 1.45] with dy*0.006

truck_model.construction: All from Three.js BoxGeometry, CylinderGeometry, RingGeometry primitives — NO external 3D model files
truck_model.material_helper: const M = (hex, rough=0.55, met=0.45) => new THREE.MeshStandardMaterial({ color: hex, roughness: rough, metalness: met })

parts.frame: Two BoxGeometry(0.12,0.18,7.8) rails at x=±0.85, y=0.27 in M(0x141414). Four cross members BoxGeometry(2.2,0.12,0.18) at z=-2.4,-0.5,1.5,3.0.
parts.windshield: BoxGeometry(1.9,0.88,0.06) at y=1.65 z=-1.32, MeshStandardMaterial({color:0x0a1420, roughness:0.05, metalness:0.15, transparent:true, opacity:0.75})
parts.rear_window: BoxGeometry(1.7,0.5,0.06) at y=1.72 z=-3.6, same transparent mat as windshield
parts.headlights: Two BoxGeometry(0.38,0.2,0.09) at x=±0.68 y=1.0 z=-3.63, MeshStandardMaterial({color:0xffffff,emissive:0x999999,emissiveIntensity:0.35})
parts.drl_strips: Two BoxGeometry(0.38,0.05,0.09) at x=±0.68 y=1.14 z=-3.63, MeshStandardMaterial({color:0xffffff,emissive:0xaaaaaa,emissiveIntensity:0.5})
parts.taillights: Two BoxGeometry(0.28,0.18,0.07) at x=±0.76 y=0.98 z=3.67, MeshStandardMaterial({color:0x880000,emissive:0x550000,emissiveIntensity:0.6})
parts.rear_bumper: BoxGeometry(2.4,0.22,0.2) at y=0.64 z=3.76, same material
parts.side_mirrors: Two mirror bodies BoxGeometry(0.1,0.22,0.35) at x=±1.34 y=1.92 z=-1.95 + stems BoxGeometry(0.06,0.09,0.3) at x=±1.26
parts.exhaust_stacks: Two CylinderGeometry(0.055,0.07,0.85,10) at x=±0.88 y=2.5 z=-2.7 + caps CylinderGeometry(0.08,0.055,0.05,10) at y+0.45
parts.fuel_tank: CylinderGeometry(0.24,0.24,1.2,12) rotation.z=PI/2 at x=-1.22 y=0.55 z=-0.3, M(0x1e1e1e,0.7,0.5)

each_wheel: 6 lug nuts: CylinderGeometry(0.025,0.025,0.035,6) arranged in circle r=0.09 around hub, offset to outer face

parts.mud_flaps: BoxGeometry(0.06,0.45,0.35) at four positions behind front and rear wheel arches

detection_zones.description: Four flat BoxGeometry zones on the ground plane around the truck. Each has a fill mesh and an EdgesGeometry wireframe outline.
detection_zones.zone_material: MeshBasicMaterial transparent:true, side:DoubleSide — fill + LineBasicMaterial for edges
detection_zones.active_state_update: When detections state changes, compute Set of active camera_zones. For each zone: if active AND blind_zone: fillMat.opacity=0.10, edgeMat.opacity=0.85, color 0xcc2222. If active AND not blind: fillMat.opacity=0.04, edgeMat.opacity=0.3, color 0x555555. If inactive: fillMat.opacity=0, edgeMat.opacity=0.12 (blind) or 0.06 (front), color dimmed.

detection_blips.description: For each active detection, render a sphere + ring at the corresponding zone position with jitter based on detection id
detection_blips.sphere: SphereGeometry(0.12,8,8), MeshBasicMaterial — red (0xcc2222) for blind zones, gray (0x555555) for front. Position: zone base + jitter from sin/cos of detection.id.
detection_blips.ring: RingGeometry(0.2,0.26,16), MeshBasicMaterial transparent opacity 0.4, rotation.x=-PI/2, flat on ground
detection_blips.lifecycle: Remove all previous blip meshes from scene, add new ones. Store refs in a blipsRef array.

overlay_labels: Top-left: 'LIVE · 3D SURROUND VIEW' — position absolute, z-index 10, var(--fc) 10px 600 letter-spacing 2.5px uppercase var(--t3)
Bottom-center above buttons: 'DRAG TO ROTATE · SCROLL TO ZOOM' — same style, opacity 0.7
ThreeJsScene.view_buttons: 4 buttons (360° | REAR | LEFT | RIGHT) at bottom-center of scene, semi-transparent dark bg. Active button: background rgba(204,34,34,0.15) border var(--red) color var(--red).

panel_header.title: 'Live Detections' — var(--fc) 10px 700 letter-spacing 3px uppercase var(--t3)
panel_header.count: detections.length — var(--fm) 28px 700 var(--t0), with ' objects' suffix in 13px var(--t3)

tabs.active_style: color var(--t0), border-bottom 2px solid var(--t0). For danger tab when active: color var(--red), border-bottom var(--red).

detection_card.normal: background var(--bg-2), border 1px solid var(--line), border-radius 8px
detection_card.layout: Two rows: top row = [emoji + object type uppercase | confidence %], bottom row = [zone dot + zone name | time ago]
detection_card.confidence_badge: var(--fm) 11px 700, background var(--bg-3), border 1px var(--line), border-radius 4px. If confidence > 0.8: color var(--t0), else color var(--t2).
detection_card.zone_dot: 6px circle — red for left/right/rear, var(--t3) for front
detection_card.time_ago: Derive from detection.timestamp: now if <2s, '{n}s' if <60s, '{n}m' otherwise. Update on each render tick or every second.
detection_card.animation: slide-in keyframe on mount: opacity 0 → 1, translateY -4px → 0, 0.2s ease

cells.label: CONF AVG
cells.value: Average confidence as '{n}%' or '—' if no detections

stats_footer.cell_style: padding 12px 16px, border-right 1px var(--line) (last: none). Label: var(--fc) 9px 700 letter-spacing 2.5px uppercase var(--t3). Value: var(--fm) 18px 700 var(--t0). Red class: var(--red). Dim class: var(--t2) 14px.

SettingsModal.overlay: Fixed full-screen rgba(0,0,0,0.7), z-index 1050, flex center
SettingsModal.box: background var(--bg-2), border 1px var(--line), border-radius 12px, max-width 400px, padding 24px
SettingsModal.title: 'Server Configuration' — var(--fc) 16px 700 uppercase letter-spacing 2px
SettingsModal.content: Label 'WEBSOCKET HOST' + text input with current serverIP value
SettingsModal.input_style: width 100%, background var(--bg-3), border 1px var(--line), border-radius 6px, color var(--t0), font-family var(--fm), font-size 14px, padding 10px 14px
SettingsModal.footer: Two buttons: 'CANCEL' (ghost, closes modal) + 'APPLY & RECONNECT' (solid white bg black text). On apply: update serverIP state, close modal — the WebSocket useEffect watching serverIP will automatically reconnect.
SettingsModal.validation: Regex test for IPv4: /^(\d{1,3}\.){3}\d{1,3}$/ OR hostname OR 'localhost'. Show inline error if invalid.

state_management.detections: array — set by WebSocket onmessage. Default [].
state_management.isConnected: boolean — set by ws.onopen/onclose. Default false.
state_management.serverIP: string — default window.location.hostname || '192.168.1.100'. Changing this triggers WebSocket reconnect.
state_management.alertActive: boolean — true when blind spot detection received. Auto-resets after 3000ms.
state_management.fps: integer — updated every 1000ms from message rate counter.

derived_values.filteredDets: Apply activeTab filter logic to detections array

file_structure.option_a_single_file: All in src/App.js — CSS string injected via <style> tag, Three.js scene in a useEffect hook, all components as functions in same file. Simplest approach.

option_b_multi_file.src/App.js: Root component — all state, WebSocket logic, layout assembly
option_b_multi_file.src/hooks/useWebSocket.js: WebSocket hook — manages connection, reconnect, message parsing, fps counter
option_b_multi_file.src/hooks/useThreeScene.js: Three.js scene setup, truck model, zone meshes, blips — accepts detections + cameraView as params
option_b_multi_file.src/components/SidePanel.jsx: Side panel with tabs + detection list

implementation_constraints: Do NOT import OrbitControls or any @react-three/drei — implement orbit manually with mouse events
Do NOT use react-three-fiber — use THREE directly with a canvas ref
Do NOT use localStorage or sessionStorage
Do NOT add any colors other than white, gray, black, and #cc2222 red
Do NOT add glassmorphism (no backdrop-filter blur on panels)
Do NOT add looping glow/pulse animations on elements other than the connection dot and alert blinking dots
Three.js lights must be white/neutral only — no colored tints
WebSocket must reconnect automatically on close after 3 seconds
All Three.js objects must be properly disposed on component unmount to prevent memory leaks
ResizeObserver must be used to keep canvas resolution correct on resize
Pixel ratio must be capped at 2 for performance

replace_mock_data.instruction: The reference implementation uses setInterval with Math.random() to simulate detections. REMOVE ALL of this. Replace with the WebSocket integration described above. The only data source for detections, connection status, and fps must be the live WebSocket connection.
remove: Any setInterval that generates random detections
Any Math.random() calls for detection data
keep: The fps setInterval (reading from msgCountRef — this is legitimate)
